summary:
Following professional cleaning and pumicing, plaque formation initiates almost instantly due to residual bacteria and salivary components facilitating rapid reattachment. While initial layers may not become visibly noticeable right away, measurable changes occur within minutes as microbes adhere to freshly cleansed surfaces. This aligns with findings suggesting that significant plaque development generally occurs between thirty minutes to one hour post-procedure, influenced by environmental factors like saliva flow rate and bacterial load remaining in the oral cavity despite prior cleansing efforts.
